title: FuelCell Energy Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Fiscal Year 2024 Results
text: FuelCell Energy reported Q4 FY2024 (ended Oct 31, 2024) revenue of $49.3M, up 120% YoY from $22.5M. Gross loss was $(10.9)M vs $(1.5)M prior year. Net loss per share was $(2.21) vs $(2.07). Adjusted EBITDA was $(25.3)M vs $(30.8)M. Revenue breakdown: Product $25.4M (primarily $18M from South Korean modules for Gyeonggi Green Energy), Service $5.6M, Generation $12.0M (up 40.3%), Advanced Technologies $6.4M. Full fiscal year 2024 revenue was $112.1M (down 9% from $123.4M). Gross loss was $(35.9)M vs $(10.5)M. Net loss per share was $(7.83) vs $(7.92). Adjusted EBITDA was $(101.1)M vs $(102.9)M. Cash and investments totaled $318.0M (down from $403.3M). Total backlog was $1.16B (up 13.1%). Total assets were $944.1M. CEO Jason Few noted revenue more than doubled YoY, mainly driven by module sales to Gyeonggi Green Energy in South Korea. The company announced a 13% workforce reduction and plans 15% operating cost cuts in FY2025.
